paper_title: Analytical and Sample Collection Methods in Marine Sediment Study

methods.description: Determination of dissolved thiosulfate concentration in pore water samples using ion chromatography.

steps.description: Dilute pore water samples 1000-fold with deionized water and pass through 0.22 µm hydrophilic polyvinylidene fluoride membrane filters.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute and filter pore water samples

steps.description: Determine thiosulfate concentration using ion chromatography with an Eco IC equipped with a conductivity detector and a Metrosep A Supp5 -250/4.0 anion exchange column.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ion chromatography to measure thiosulfate concentration

steps.description: Prepare calibration curve using analytical-grade thiosulfate IC Standard and measure three different concentrations (0.5, 5, and 20 µM) for calibration.
steps.searchable_summary: Create calibration curve for thiosulfate quantification

methods.description: Measurement of tetrathionate concentration in pore water samples using the cyanolytic method.

steps.description: Add 0.1 mL of pore water sample to 0.8 mL of cyanolytic buffer and make up the volume to 2.0 mL.
Chill the mixture on ice for 20 min, add 1 mL of 0.1 M pre-chilled potassium cyanide, mix rapidly, and incubate on ice for another 20 min.
Add 0.6 mL of ferric nitrate reagent, allow to warm to room temperature, and measure optical density of ferric thiocyanate on a spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure optical density for tetrathionate quantification

steps.description: Prepare standard curve for tetrathionate estimation using different concentrations (0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, and 10.0 µM) of analytical grade tetrathionate.
steps.searchable_summary: Create standard curve for tetrathionate quantification

methods.description: Collection and preservation of sediment samples from the eastern Arabian Sea OMZ for biogeochemical analysis.

steps.description: Collect gravity cores from water depths of 225 to 1275 m b.s.l. during exploration of the eastern Arabian Sea OMZ.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment cores from specified depths

steps.description: Remove 30 cm long C-shaped part of PVC core liner, shower with high-purity N2, and scrape off the top 1 cm of the exposed surface.
Scoop out 5 mm thick sediment slice for microbiological studies and store in sterile polypropylene bottles.
Flush sample bottles with high-purity N2, seal with Parafilm, and store at specified temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve sediment samples under controlled conditions

steps.description: Extract pore waters using sterile syringes, centrifuge, and filter supernatants for chemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and prepare pore water for analysis
